title: C extension (_speedups.c) analysis: 15 bugs found
author: devdanzin
body:
## Summary

We ran an automated C extension analysis on `simplejson/_speedups.c` (v3.20.2, 3,414 lines) using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit) (13 specialized agents covering refcount safety, error paths, NULL safety, GC, type slots, and more).

**Full report with details and reproducers:** https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e8a25ea988dc0461c2fd378efd84a8a3

### Results: 15 FIX-level bugs, 8 reproduced

| # | Finding | Severity | Reproduced? |
|---|---------|----------|-------------|
| 14 | Double `Py_XDECREF` on `ident` → use-after-free segfault | Critical | **YES** (segfault) |
| 15 | `ident` leak on `Py_EnterRecursiveCall` failure | High | **YES** (158.5 bytes/error) |
| 1 | `maybe_quote_bigint` error-as-truthy → abort on debug | High | **YES** (abort) |
| 11 | `is_raw_json` error-as-truthy | High | Code-confirmed |
| 3 | `iterable_as_array` swallows MemoryError/KeyboardInterrupt | High | **YES** |
| 7 | Variable shadowing leaks `encoded` in dict encoder | Medium | Code-confirmed |
| 13 | skipkeys `item` leak (14.5 bytes per skipped key) | Medium | **YES** (tracemalloc) |
| 4 | `moduleinit` NULL deref on OOM | Medium | Code-confirmed |
| 8 | `_encoded_const` `Py_INCREF(NULL)` on lazy init failure | Medium | Code-confirmed |
| 9 | Partial static init leaves NULL statics | Medium | Code-confirmed |
| 2 | `raise_errmsg` loses exception on OOM | Medium | Code-confirmed |
| 5 | `encoder_traverse` missing `Py_VISIT(skipkeys_bool)` | Low | Code-confirmed |
| 6 | `encoder_members` maps `"encoding"` to wrong field (copy-paste) | Low | **YES** |
| 12 | `PyLong_AsLong` exception clobbering (OverflowError → TypeError) | Low | **YES** |
| 10 | `int_as_string_bitcount` parity gap (C vs Python encoder) | Low | Code-confirmed |

### Individual issues

We've filed separate issues grouped by bug class, each with reproducers and suggested fixes:

- #349 — Crash: use-after-free and leak in `ident` handling (Findings 14, 15)
- #350 — Error-as-truthy bugs in `maybe_quote_bigint` and `is_raw_json` (Findings 1, 11)
- #351 — Exception swallowing in `iterable_as_array` (Finding 3)
- #352 — Reference leaks in dict encoder (Findings 7, 13)
- #353 — NULL dereferences on OOM (Findings 4, 8, 9)
- #354 — Minor: member table copy-paste, exception clobbering, traverse (Findings 5, 6, 12)

### Additional CONSIDER items (not filed as issues)

- `raise_errmsg` (Finding 2) loses exception on OOM — low-priority since it requires OOM during exception construction
- `int_as_string_bitcount` parity gap (Finding 10) — C encoder rejects values >= 64 but Python encoder accepts any positive integer
- 3 `PyDict_GetItem` calls that silently swallow lookup errors — should migrate to `PyDict_GetItemWithError`
- `PyModule_AddObject` → `PyModule_AddObjectRef` migration
- `structmember.h` deprecated since 3.12

### Strengths noted

The codebase has excellent fundamentals: zero scanner-flagged refcount bugs (all 31 candidates were false positives), consistent `goto bail` cleanup, proper GC support, and subclassing correctly blocked. The bugs hide in error paths and callback interactions.

Found using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

comments.author: etrepum
comments.body: Do you intend to submit PRs for these issues as well?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Yes, in the process of creating them now.

comments.author: etrepum
comments.body: I think all of these should be addressed
